entry.title: Your AI Models Just Got a Hardware Upgrade
entry.content:
Co-Author: Lukas Pätz

TL;DR

SAP AI Core (the engine) now supports flexible instance types — pick the exact CPU or GPU your workload needs
New instance types deliver up to 4.5–5x the compute performance of old resource plans
Access the latest GPUs (L4, L40S, H100) without waiting for SAP to package them into plans
Your existing resource plans still work — instance types are opt-in where they help

Start Here: SAP Note 3660109

Why This Matters

If you're running your custom AI models on SAP's Generative AI Hub (technical service SAP AI Core), this one's for you.

We've heard the feedback: newer GPUs are available from hyperscalers, but you can't use them yet because SAP hasn't released a matching resource plan. That lag is frustrating — especially when the difference between a V100 and an H100 isn't incremental. It's generational. We're talking 4.5–5x the compute performance for the right workloads. An inference call that takes 8 seconds on older hardware might finish in under 1 second on newer chips. That's the gap between "possible in theory" and "shipping in production."

Starting now, that gap is closing.
 

What Changed: Resource Plans → Instance Types

Think of the old model like a set menu at a restaurant. Curated, predictable, but limited to what the chef decided to offer.

Instance types are the shift to à la carte. You see the full range of CPUs and GPUs available across cloud providers, and you pick exactly what your workload needs — from a lightweight CPU for preprocessing to an H100 for training large models.

Resource plans aren't going away. They still work. But instance types are now the primary path for getting the newest, most capable hardware into your deployments — faster, and with finer control than the old model allowed. We highly recommend to leverage instance types going forward (only available in the "extended" service plan, now be the right time to migrate from standard to extended)

Here's the point: you tell us what your model needs; we make sure the right compute is there. Bon appétit.

What You Actually Get

A quick clarification: If you're using pre-integrated foundation models through SAP's Generative AI Hub — GPT-5.2, Claude 4.6 Opus or Gemini — this update doesn't directly affect you. Those models are already managed and optimized; you just call the API.

Instance types matter when you're running your own models: custom fine-tuned models, your proprietary models (like team-built solutions), embedding models for RAG pipelines, or specialized computer vision and industry models. That's when hardware choice becomes your concern — and when better hardware translates directly into better outcomes.

Faster inference, faster training

This isn't about spec sheets. It's about what becomes possible with the models you've built.

Custom models that were borderline viable for real-time use — domain-specific classifiers, fine-tuned embedding models, proprietary prediction models — become clearly viable when you can run them on L40S instead of T4. Training jobs that took days can finish in hours on H100. Features your team considered out of reach might now fit inside your existing budget.

L4 and L40S chips deliver substantially higher throughput than T4, at lower cost per inference call. H100 provides order-of-magnitude gains over V100 for training workloads. If you're pushing the limits of what's feasible with custom models, this matters.

Better cost efficiency — without re-architecting

Faster hardware means less compute time per job, which means lower cost. Simple as that.

Instance types also let you right-size. You stop paying for a resource plan heavier than you need. The result: better cost-per-output without touching your model or application logic.

 

New hardware when it ships — not months later

When hyperscalers release the next GPU generation, you won't be waiting for SAP to define a new resource plan. Instance types let us surface new hardware significantly faster. Your infrastructure stays current.

Multi-cloud, multi-region resilience

Instance types are available across cloud providers and regions. Deploy closer to your users or data. Meet compliance requirements. Reduce single-vendor dependency. Keep two or three validated alternatives per region and you're protected against capacity constraints.

Which Instance Should You Use?

 

WorkloadRecommended InstanceExamples
Orchestration, preprocessing, CPU-based MLCPU (AWS m7i, Azure Dv6, GCP n4)Data pipelines, API routing, lightweight model serving
Custom model inference (high throughput)L4 / L40S GPU (AWS g6, g6e)Fine-tuned classifiers, embedding models, custom vision models
Fine-tuning and training medium-to-large modelsA100 (GCP A2 Ultra)Domain-specific model fine-tuning, complex custom inference
Training large language modelsH100 (Azure NC H100 v5)Custom LLM training, proprietary foundation model development

Match the instance to your workload. Account for regional availability. Keep at least two alternatives validated per region.

Use the compute factors in SAP Note 3660109 or the SAP Cost Calculator to compare options on a consistent basis — each instance type maps to a compute factor in node-hours, which makes cost planning straightforward.

Migration: No Big Bang Required

Both options work today. You don't have to migrate everything at once. More details:

# This still works
ai.sap.com/resourcePlan: "infer.s"

# This is the new option — use where it helps
ai.sap.com/instanceType: "g6.4xlarge"

Start with the workloads where newer hardware will have the clearest impact — typically your highest-volume inference jobs or your most compute-heavy training runs. Everything else can stay on resource plans, but newer hardware might be more efficient.

Full specs, regional availability, and compute factors: SAP Note 3660109.

Staying Current

Hardware in AI is moving fast — faster than enterprise software typically keeps up. Instance types are how we keep you on the right side of that curve, without adding operational overhead to your team.

To stay informed:

  • Subscribe to SAP Note 3660109 — it's the live catalogue; new instance types show up here as they become available
  • Watch the What's New page for implementation guidance and new instance families

The Bottom Line

This isn't a small tweak. It's a deliberate shift to put more control in your hands — and to get it there faster.

We handle the complexity: evaluating new hardware, qualifying it, making it available at scale. You focus on what you're actually here to do: build AI solutions that matter.

The menu just expanded. Pick your instance — and dig in.

entry.title: Building Custom Joule Skills via RFC: Guide for SAP S/4HANA and ECC Integration
entry.content:
Introduction

In today’s digital enterprise landscape, rapid access to critical business data can significantly impact operational efficiency and customer satisfaction. SAP’s AI-powered digital assistant, Joule, offers organizations the ability to interact with their enterprise systems through natural language conversations. By extending Joule with custom skills, businesses can create tailored experiences that bring real-time data directly to their teams—without the need for complex navigation through traditional ERP interfaces.

This article provides a comprehensive technical guide for building custom Joule skills that connect to SAP S/4HANA or ECC systems via RFC (Remote Function Call). We’ll explore a practical use case and walk through the complete configuration process.

Business Scenario

Consider a common challenge faced by sales and operations teams: A customer inquires about the manufacturing progress of their order. Traditionally, the specialist would need to log into the ERP system, navigate through multiple screens, and manually locate the production order details. This process is time-consuming and interrupts the flow of customer communication.

With a custom Joule skill, this same specialist can simply ask Joule in natural language—"What’s the status of production order 12345?"—and receive immediate, formatted information directly within SAP Build Workzone. This streamlined approach enhances productivity and improves response times to customer inquiries.

 

 

Solution Architecture

The technical architecture comprises several integrated SAP components:

  • SAP Build Workzone: The user interface where specialists interact with Joule
  • Joule Studio: The development environment for designing and building custom AI skills
  • SAP Build: Used to create Action Projects that expose backend functionality
  • SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP): Hosts destination configurations and orchestrates connections
  • SAP Cloud Connector: Establishes secure RFC connections between BTP and on-premise or private cloud systems
  • SAP S/4HANA / ECC: The backend ERP system containing production order data

 

Configuration Steps

Step 1: Establish RFC Connectivity

The foundation of this solution is a secure connection between your cloud and on-premise environments.

Build RFC Connection with SAP Cloud Connector:
Configure the SAP Cloud Connector to create a secure tunnel between your BTP environment and your S/4HANA or ECC system. This ensures that backend functions can be accessed securely without exposing your entire system to the internet.

 

Setup Destination in BTP Cockpit:
Create a destination in your BTP subaccount that references the RFC connection established through the Cloud Connector. This destination will be consumed by SAP Build to enable communication with your backend system.

 

Step 2: Create a BAPI/RFC Action Project

With connectivity established, the next step is to expose the specific backend functionality that Joule will use.

Create the Action Project:
Navigate to the SAP Build cockpit and create a new BAPI/RFC Action project. Search for the BAPI that retrieves production order information—for example, BAPIs related to production order management.

 

 

 

 

 

 

Test the Connection:
After creating the action project, test the connection to verify that data can be successfully retrieved from your ERP system. By default, these actions operate in READ ONLY mode to protect your business data from unintended modifications.

 

 

Optional Verification with SE37:
For deeper technical validation, you can use transaction code SE37 in your S/4HANA or ECC system to directly test the BAPI function with various parameters. This helps you understand the data structures and responses before integrating them into your Joule skill.

 

 

Step 3: Design Skills in Joule Studio

This is where you design the conversational experience and connect it to your backend data.

Skill 1: Get Production Orders

Each skill consists of three major components:

  1. Skill Description: Define the purpose of the skill and the natural language triggers that will activate it when users interact with Joule.
  2. BAPI Action: Link the skill to the BAPI/RFC Action project you created in SAP Build, establishing the connection between the conversational interface and your backend data.
  3. Send Message: Design the end-user output using the Message Configuration editor. This allows you to transform raw BAPI data into formatted, interactive cards that present information clearly and professionally.

 

 

 

Skill 2: Get Production Order Detail

Following the same pattern, create a second skill that provides detailed information about a specific production order. This allows users to drill down from a list view to detailed information through natural conversation.

 

 

 

Step 4: Deploy and Enable in Workzone

The final phase makes your custom skills accessible to end users.

Create a Shared Environment:
In Joule Studio, create a Shared environment that will serve as the runtime for your deployed skills.

 

 

Deploy Your Joule Project:
Deploy your Joule project to the Shared environment. Once deployed, verify that the project appears under Custom Capabilities.

 

 

Configure Joule in SAP Build Workzone:
Set up the integration between Joule and your SAP Build Workzone site. After completing the integration configuration, enable Joule within your specific Workzone site.

 

 

Test Your Custom Capabilities:
Access your Workzone site, open the Joule client, and test your new custom AI capabilities by asking questions in natural language about production orders.

 

 

Benefits and Outcomes

By implementing custom Joule skills, organizations can achieve:

  • Increased Productivity: Eliminate time-consuming navigation through complex ERP interfaces
  • Improved Customer Service: Enable faster, more accurate responses to customer inquiries
  • Enhanced User Experience: Provide intuitive, conversational access to critical business data
  • Secure Data Access: Maintain enterprise-grade security while democratizing data access
  • Reduced Training Requirements: Lower the learning curve for accessing ERP information

Conclusion

Building custom Joule skills via RFC represents a powerful approach to modernizing how users interact with SAP systems. By combining the conversational AI capabilities of Joule with the deep business logic in S/4HANA or ECC, organizations can create intelligent, context-aware experiences that transform daily operations.

The technical implementation, while requiring careful configuration across multiple components, provides a robust and secure foundation for extending SAP’s AI capabilities to meet specific business needs. As organizations continue their digital transformation journeys, solutions like these demonstrate how AI can be practically applied to solve real business challenges.

Additional Resources

  • Build Your First Joule Skill in Joule Studio
  • Joule Studio CodeJam
  • Build custom Joule skills for SAP and non-SAP systems using Joule Studio

entry.title: From insight to impact: The future of planning 2.0 with SAP agent driven experiences
entry.content:
Planning.  But faster.  Smarter.  Way more flexible.

We’re bringing you a first look at what’s next: agent-driven planning in SAP Analytics Cloud.

Here’s the deal, intelligent agents that accelerate forecasting, a simulation layer that lets you iterate freely (finally), and clear partner opportunities across advisory, extensions, and packaged solutions.

No fluff.  Just what’s changing, why it matters, and how you can turn it into value, fast.

Plus, a preview of what’s coming ahead of SAP Sapphire.

If you’re in EPM, xP&A, or anything planning-related… you’ll want in.

Register now.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/mar/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00013550.html

entry.title: VNSG SAP BTP Hackathon 2026: Champions Crowned at SYNC DemoJam
entry.content:
The Stage is Set
The VNSG SAP BTP Hackathon 2026 reached its spectacular grand finale on the main stage of VNSG SYNC 2026 – the annual VNSG and SAP Netherlands year event. In front of a packed keynote audience on 12 March, three finalist teams had exactly five minutes to demonstrate the business impact of their SAP BTP-powered innovations. The audience then cast their votes – and the results were electric.

From Challenge to Champion: The Journey
The hackathon launched in late 2025 when VNSG, SAP BTP Product Management and SAP NL Presales invited Dutch SAP customers and partners to submit real-life business challenges. Six teams were selected and, after a structured enablement phase, spent weeks co-building solutions with dedicated coaches. In February 2026, all six teams pitched to a jury panel, which selected three finalists for the grand DemoJam finale at SYNC. The three finalists stood out for their clear business relevance, technical ambition and the real-world potential to take their prototypes to production.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Champion: Promocean + SOA People — Purchase Order Control Center
Luc van Elk and Thomas Langhout from Promocean, supported by SOA People, took the audience vote and claimed the top spot. Their solution – the Purchase Order Control Center – puts SAP Business AI at the heart of procurement operations.
By connecting AI directly to SAP ECC, the team demonstrated how intelligent automation can dramatically reduce manual effort, accelerate purchase order processing, and surface exceptions before they become costly problems. A showcase of how AI on SAP BTP can deliver operational excellence in one of the most common – and pain-prone – business processes in any enterprise.

Runner-up: Ecotone + Expertum — Integration Suite Management
Arjan de Mol and Bart van de Kamp from Ecotone, partnered with Expertum Consulting, earned second place with a solution that tackles a pain many integration teams know all too well: managing and controlling large numbers of iFlows across landscapes. Their Integration Suite Management solution brings Joule-powered automation to iFlow lifecycle management – enabling operators to undeploy and deploy groups of iFlows intelligently. This turns a time-consuming, error-prone manual process into a governed, AI-assisted workflow, giving integration teams transparency, stability and control. 

Third Place: NTT DATA Business Solutions — ProTrack
Laurens Steffers and Jorrit van der Sanden from NTT DATA Business Solutions presented ProTrack – a solution delivering real-time insight for machines on the production floor. Built on SAP BTP, ProTrack addresses a critical operational need in manufacturing: knowing what is happening on your shop floor right now, not minutes later. By streaming machine data into an SAP BTP-powered dashboard, teams can react faster to exceptions, reduce downtime and optimize throughput.

 

Beyond the Podium: A Strong Field
The jury had a hard job. Aarini Consulting, Nederlandse Spoorwegen (NS), and Simac IT & BSC also built
impressive solutions that demonstrated real business value on SAP BTP. Every team that reached the jury pitch proved
that you can go from business challenge to working prototype in a short time - when you combine the right platform
with the right people. A sincere congratulations to all six participating teams !!

Behind the Scenes
Want to experience the atmosphere of the DemoJam from backstage? Watch the behind-the-scenes video - including a
live demo of how SAP Business AI is changing the way people interact with SAP, presented by Jason Enti and Ronald
Schippers: Behind the scenes at the VNSG SAP BTP DemoJam SYNC 2026

The People Who Made it Happen
A hackathon like this does not happen by itself. A huge thank you to everyone who contributed:
Presented at SYNC by: Twan van den Broek and Irene Rompa
Team coaches: Jan Penninkhof , Alex van den Hoek and Twan van den Broek
Jury: Ronald Schippersm Steven Nuyts, Steffen Schad, Jonathan Freeland
Made possible by: Mientje Paais, Olaf Winkler, Judy Bui and Niklas Siemer

What This Proves
The VNSG SAP BTP Hackathon 2026 is proof that innovation does not require a blank slate or a greenfield project. It requires the right platform, the right people and a real problem worth solving.
SAP BTP – combining SAP Integration Suite, SAP Build, SAP HANA Cloud, SAP Business AI and cloud-native runtimes – gives teams everything they need to move from prototype to production at speed.
The finalist solutions were not demos for the sake of demos. They addressed real procurement pain, real integration
complexity and real manufacturing visibility gaps. And they were built in weeks, not months.

What's Next?
The conversation does not end on the SYNC stage. All teams are encouraged to work with their coaches to plan production rollouts and turn hackathon prototypes into enterprise solutions.

For the broader SAP Community : Stay tuned for the next edition of the VNSG SAP BTP Hackathon. Bring your own
real-life business challenge and co-build with partners and SAP experts.
And who knows - your solution could be on the SYNC main stage in 2027.

SAP Business Technology Platform SAP Build SAP Integration Suite SAP Business AI 

@hansvp @qmrjvd @sricsi98 @BvE @mdschoenmakers @tamasszirtes @f_van_leeuwen @Petra1 @BartvdKamp @tedcastelijns @Arjan_deMol @dvvelzen @JdTeuling @AnnikaHeus @LaurensSteffers

When Innovation Meets Real Business Impact – VNSG SAP BTP Hackathon 2026
Grand Finale VNSG SAP BTP Hackathon in a DemoJam

entry.title: Clean Core for Utilities: The Missing Link Between S/4HANA and AI Driven Operations
entry.content:
Clean Core for Utilities: The Missing Link Between S/4HANA and AI‑Driven Operations

 

Most of the Utilities organization are entering one of the most disruptive decades the industry has ever seen. Aging assets due to modern technologies, rising customer expectations due to digital world, and the rapid growth of renewables are forcing companies to rethink how they operate and manage their system. Many utilities organizations have already started their S/4HANA programs with the hope that modernization will naturally lead to automation, intelligence, and AI-driven insights.

But here’s the reality most teams discover the hard way:

AI doesn’t scale on a messy foundation. And without a Clean Core, S/4HANA alone won’t get you there.

Across utilities in the U.S., APAC, Europe and the Middle East, the organizations making real progress but having  one thing in common: they aren’t just migrating — they’re rebuilding their architectural backbone from scratch of the system or existing design so AI can deliver business value the way they want it to get modern success.

Why Utilities Are Still Struggling

Many utilities organizations are carrying 15–25 years of technical debt. Their ECC  or R3 and IS‑U systems often include:

  • Custom code buried in user exits, BADI, FQ Event and enhancements both implicit and explicit.
  • Hard‑coded logic only a developer or few team member understand.
  • Manual steps scattered across the meter‑to‑cash cycle.
  • Data spread across GIS, AMI, OMS, CIS and legacy and other third-party tools.

The result is a fragmented landscape where every process behaves differently. And that’s the real blocker. Many utilities think their challenge is adopting AI. But the truth is:

AI cannot operate on inconsistent data or unpredictable processes.

What Clean Core Actually Means         

This important point is most of the people misconception that Clean Core means “no customization.” That’s not realistic for utilities, at least for now — regulatory and market needs are too complex, and greater flexibility is still needed, but future may change for above statement is true for now.

Clean Core is not about eliminating logic. It’s about putting logic in the right place.

A practical Clean Core approach means:

1. Keep SAP S/4HANA Utilities standard

Use it as a stable, upgrade‑safe system of record for metering, billing, finance, and core operations.

2. Move differentiation to SAP BTP

Build workflows, extensions, and apps on BTP — not inside the core.

3. Use events instead of modifications

A meter read posted in SAP S/4HANA Utilities should trigger an event. That event can activate workflows, validations, or AI agents on BTP — without touching billing logic.

Clean Core doesn’t slow innovation. It creates the conditions for innovation to scale safely.

Why Clean Core Is the Missing Link for AI

AI only works well when three things are true:

  • Data is consistent, this must have.
  • Processes are predictable, of course this is something to understandable.
  • System behavior is stable; this is bottleneck as well.

Clean Core enforces all three.

Without it, AI stays stuck in pilots’ phase. With it, AI becomes operational. Utilities that adopt Clean Core unlock outcomes which are:

  • Predictive billing to reduce customer call and dispute resolution.
  • Autonomous collections based on customer behavior. Important for utility industries.
  • DER/EV readiness without breaking billing logic, of course main source of revenue generation.
  • Revenue integrity through real‑time anomaly detection.

This shifts from reactive operations to predictive and autonomous ones and trust me this is future of utilities at least from my prospective with AI Era and digital world we are moving in.

Reference Architecture for an AI‑Ready Utility

This future architecture can be understood in three layers:

1.       Transactional Core (SAP S/4HANA Utilities)
  • System of record for metering, billing, and finance data
  • Standardized and upgrade‑friendly
  • Minimal custom code
2.       Innovation Layer (SAP BTP)

Where differentiation lives. Using:

  • Integration Suite
  • Build Process Automation
  • Event Mesh
  • AI Foundation
  • Joule Agents
  • Customer microservices

This is where utilities build intelligent workflows and automation — without touching the core.

3.       AI‑Driven Operations

The outcome layer:

  • Predictive insights
  • Automated processes
  • AI agents managing meter‑to‑cash
  • Intelligent customer interactions
  • Real‑time decisioning

This isn’t just a tech stack — it’s a new operating model.

A Practical Maturity Path: Utilities don’t need to jump to AI overnight. A realistic path looks like which I feel should be:

  • Level 1: Customized Core (Reactive): Heavy custom code, manual processes, limited visibility.
  • Level 2: Clean Core + BTP (Responsive): Standard core with side‑by‑side innovation.
  • Level 3: Event‑Driven + AI (Autonomous): Real‑time processes with AI‑driven decisioning.

The goal isn’t perfection — it’s steady movement toward a scalable, intelligent architecture.

 

 

How to Get Started

Here is practical first steps:

  1. Assess your landscape, identify custom code that can be retired or moved out of the core.
  2. Adopt standard processes first S/4HANA already solves many historical customizations.
  3. Build side‑by‑side by default innovation belongs on BTP.
  4. Prioritize data quality AI is only as good as the data feeding it.

Conclusion

Clean Core is no longer an architectural preference — it’s a prerequisite for AI‑driven utilities.

The future utility isn’t just delivering power or water or gas. It’s managing data, events, and intelligent processes in real time.

Utilities that recognize this shift early won’t just modernize — they’ll redefine how the industry operates.
